findings.evidence: Roadmap OneParameterSemigroups/README.md:27-31 ('contraction case is the corollary, never the silent default') and :112-119 (resolvent for real λ > ω, bound M/(λ−ω)); existsGrowthBound proven at Basic.lean:803 supplies M, ω.
findings.fix: Define resolvent on StronglyContinuousSemigroup for λ > ω; state the (M,ω) bound; recover the contraction 1/λ case as a corollary.
findings.issue: ContractionSemigroup.resolvent, resolvent_norm_le (785), resolventMapsToDomain (743), resolventRightInv (756) are all defined only on the contraction subclass with 0 < λ. The roadmap mandates defining the resolvent on a general C₀-semigroup for real λ > ω and stating the bound at the general (M, ω) level (‖R(λ)‖ ≤ M/(λ−ω)), with the contraction case as the corollary.

findings.evidence: ‖e^{−λt} S t x‖ ≤ M e^{(ω−λ)t}‖x‖ ∈ L¹(Ioi 0) iff λ > ω; existsGrowthBound (line 803) yields M, ω.
findings.fix: Restate integrability for λ > ω using the growth bound from existsGrowthBound; drop the contraction-only rationale.
findings.issue: The 'Why contraction?' docstring claims the contraction restriction is necessary for integrability, but it is not: over λ > ω the integrand bound M·e^{(ω−λ)t}·‖x‖ is integrable. The justification defends an under-generalization rather than addressing it.

summary: The semigroup, generator, and growth-bound API sit at the correct general C₀ level, but the entire resolvent API (definition, norm bound, maps-to-domain, right-inverse) is specialized to the ContractionSemigroup subclass — the silent specialization the roadmap's generality bar forbids. The general λ > ω resolvent is constructible from the in-file existsGrowthBound.
